insight: Bombarder seems overly strong and is purchased automatically even without clear purpose, suggesting it may be overcosted for its power level.
author: mqp
context: Player discussing unit balance in early July 2018.

insight: Bombarder at 9BBR is situationally valuable primarily when it can absorb damage as part of a Wall+Bombarder combination; otherwise its value is unclear.
author: redrame
context: Discussion of Bombarder viability in specific unit combinations.

insight: Mega Drone is the best red absorber in the game due to its ability to absorb in burst turns and lategame without committing to double blue tech.
author: darkeisbein, swapgo
context: Evaluation of Mega Drone's role compared to Wall and other red absorbers.

insight: Blood Phage is cost-efficient compared to a fast Tarsier (costed at roughly 5R) with the added benefit of converting excess red into gold through its ability.
author: swapgo, cogito3, shadourow
context: Discussion of Blood Phage's efficiency and role in red-heavy sets.

insight: Cluster Bolt at its previous cost of 5/2 was stronger overall, particularly in non-mirror matchups, but weaker in scenarios where both players care equally about damage.
author: mrguy888, amalloy
context: Historical balance discussion comparing old and new Cluster Bolt costs.

insight: Grimbotch is significantly higher value than Tarsier when it can deal full damage without absorb denial, forcing opponents to spend on soak, but only if the player switches to Grimbotches once opponent attackers threaten.
author: Deleted User, swapgo
context: Replay analysis discussing unit selection and timing between Tarsiers and Grimbotches.

insight: Holding Arka against freeze threats (Vai) is generally better than attacking with it because forcing opponent to rebuild 4 soak provides a tempo advantage of approximately half a turn.
author: leary93, extratricky
context: Discussion of optimal play when facing freeze-based removal.

insight: Clicking Arka is preferred when the absorber is Vigilant (cannot soak), or when significantly ahead and opponent has no immediate threat, because attacking converts threat into real damage.
author: swapgo, leary93
context: Strategic guidelines for when to attack vs hold Arka.

insight: Holding Arka provides inflation value because opponent must spend gold to rebuild defense while holder does not rebuild, creating a tempo swing that outweighs the direct 4 attack versus 4 absorb tradeoff.
author: leary93
context: Explanation of economic advantage from holding Arka.

insight: Drake clicking decision depends primarily on whether 2 damage now is worth more than a Blastforge (or 5 gold to replace it), with consideration for opponent's response commitment.
author: xyotsuba, shadourow
context: Discussion of Drake click valuation and opportunity cost.

insight: Drake is often thought of as a 'fake threat' that rebates cost through extra enemy defense purchases; the true value is comparing 2 damage now against the defense overhead it forces.
author: totally_not_fbi
context: Conceptual framework for valuing Drake threats.

insight: Wild Drone is worth 1 gold less than double Drone but enables turn 2 Animus on player 2, which represents a massive tempo advantage even when opponent can snipe the wilds.
author: mwortel, supergreyman
context: Discussion of Wild Drone value in enabling early Animus.

insight: The 3 gold per Conduit rule of thumb accounts for spending green on mixed-cost units and avoiding excessive banking; accuracy depends on set composition.
author: consensus
context: Explanation and validation of tech cost heuristic.

insight: Buying Conduit specifically for Forcefields should generally only happen in lategame when you would rather have defense than drones, to avoid wasting green or bleeding drones early.
author: xyotsuba, cogito3
context: Guidance on timing Conduit purchase for Forcefield production.

insight: High inflation game states favor immediate-value units (Rhino, Grimbotch) because the game ends sooner and tempo matters more than long-term economy; low inflation favors delayed-value units (Tarsier).
author: swapgo, supergreyman, miuna6933
context: Explanation of unit selection based on game inflation state.

insight: Rhino train is a high-inflation timing play where multiple Rhinos are built in advance to use stamina and soak efficiently when pressure becomes heavy; requires reading several turns ahead.
author: supergreyman
context: Description of Rhino train tactic in high-inflation scenarios.

insight: Chieftain train requires predicting 3+ turns ahead to ensure 7+ absorb value; setup costs one Blastforge 3 turns in advance and usually requires a second Conduit, making it tricky but powerful.
author: supergreyman, swapgo
context: Analysis of Chieftain train setup and resource requirements.

insight: Redeemer should almost never be used to absorb (absorbing with it is 3 attack for 1 absorb, poor ratio); better to gambit for small breach than block with it.
author: swapgo, supergreyman
context: Discussion of optimal Redeemer usage and when not to block with it.

insight: Buying an Animus without good red attackers to spend the Replicase is generally a mistake; if there are no strong red units, reconsider buying Animus at all.
author: cogito3, swapgo
context: Guidance on avoiding wasteful Animus purchases.

insight: Having thing one turn earlier is significantly more effective than having it one turn later due to compounding economy and attack; thus buy drones early to enable future purchases.
author: swapgo
context: Fundamental principle explaining tempo advantage.

insight: Buy defense only at the last moment because money not spent on defense can fund more attackers or drones; buying Walls, Forcefields, or Aegis early is floating gold.
author: swapgo
context: Principle of minimizing defense spending to maximize tempo.

insight: Deadeye Operative should not be rushed to attack Wild Drones in sets where Wild Drones are abundant, because the opponent will simply avoid keeping drones as snipe targets.
author: cactus_wren
context: Discussion of how Deadeye Operative becomes ineffective in certain meta.

insight: Snipe targets should not be prioritized in low inflation if it doesn't deny opponent absorb; instead maintain pressure on defenses until opponent invests heavily, then snipe to make defense values bad.
author: amalloy, Deleted User
context: Strategic target prioritization in games with snipe mechanics.

insight: Sniping opponent's overtech units (forcing them to carry too much tech) adds value beyond the drone killed; coordinating snipes with high-value targets can be worth pursuing.
author: hologoddessofgrainandapples
context: Advanced snipe targeting considering opponent's tech load.

insight: DD DB opening (when both Drones are on turn 2 followed by Drone and Blastforge on turn 3) is flexible and refutes stranger P2 aggressive openings, providing multiple build paths.
author: leary93, .bky_1556, cactus_wren
context: Discussion of defensive opening build order.

insight: Wall absorption of exactly 1 damage is worth buying when no alternative absorb is available, contrary to intuition, to prevent opponent from gaining tempo through free shots.
author: Deleted User, consensus
context: Clarification that minimal absorb is still valuable.
